raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased an item from XXXX XXXX for approximately {$2000.00} on XX/XX/year>, which was delivered on XX/XX/year>. According to XXXX XXXX posted return policy ( attached ), returns are accepted within 30 days of receipt. 

I initiated the return on XX/XX/year> just 26 days after receiving the order and have proof of shipment ( tracking label attached ). XXXX XXXX received the return on XX/XX/year>. Despite this, they refused to process the refund, claiming that the return arrived after 30 days. Their written policy, however, does not require that returns arrive within 30 days, only that they be initiated ( sent ) within that period. I have no control over carriers shipping times and how long it might take.

I filed a dispute with American Express, providing full documentation
: The merchants return policy Order receipt and delivery confirmation My return shipping tracking showing XX/XX/year> American Express denied the dispute, siding with the merchant, even though the evidence clearly shows I complied with the return policy. Amex did not provide a meaningful explanation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) or offer a proper reinvestigation.

This situation unfairly shifts the burden of the merchants internal delay to me, despite my compliance. I believe American Express failed to protect me under the FCBA, which requires issuers to conduct a reasonable investigation when goods are returned according to the merchants stated policy.
